title: FAB Button
subtitle: Floating action button with radial layouts and multiple customization options

basic.title: Basic
basic.description: The simplest usage of the FAB Button: expands actions upward in a linear layout when the trigger is clicked.

directions.title: Directions
directions.description: Control where items expand with direction. Available values: up, down, left, right and the four diagonals.

layouts.title: Layouts
layouts.description: Define the spatial distribution of items with layout: linear (inline), semi-circle (semicircle), quarter-circle (quarter circle) and circle (full circle).

shapes.title: Shapes
shapes.description: Customize the border radius of the trigger and items with shape: circular, rounded or square.

colors.title: Colors
colors.description: Use any of the 8 semantic system colors with the color input.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Control the size of the trigger and items with size: xs, sm, md (default), lg and xl.

animation.title: Animations
animation.description: Choose how items enter and exit with animation: scale (default), fade or slide.

trigger.title: Trigger
trigger.description: Customize the trigger button with triggerIcon, triggerIconOpen (alternative icon when open), triggerLabel (Extended FAB pattern) and triggerBadge to display a counter.

loading.title: Loading state
loading.description: Shows a spinner on the trigger with [loading]="true". While active, the trigger does not open or close the dial.

openOn.title: Open mode
openOn.description: Control how the dial opens: openOn="click" (default) or openOn="hover" for a speed-dial activated on hover. Keyboard navigation works the same in both modes.

itemDisplay.title: Item display
itemDisplay.description: itemDisplay="icon" shows only the icon with a tooltip on hover (default). itemDisplay="icon-text" shows the icon and the item's label property directly visible inside the button.

closeOptions.title: Close options
closeOptions.description: Control when the dial closes: [closeOnItemClick]="false" keeps the dial open after clicking an item (useful for multiple selection), [closeOnOutsideClick]="true" closes on outside click, [closeOnScroll]="true" closes on scroll.

backdrop.title: Backdrop
backdrop.description: Adds a translucent layer behind the items with [backdrop]="true", ideal for highlighting actions over dense content.

templates.title: Custom templates
templates.description: Project your own content into the trigger with fabTrigger and into each item with fabItem. Both directives expose the item context via let-item and the dial open state via let-open="isOpen".

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: Input properties to configure the behavior, appearance and layout of the FAB Button.

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.description: Events emitted by the FAB Button component to respond to user interactions.

computed.title: Computed Properties
computed.description: Reactive computed properties (Signals) that expose the resolved internal state of the component.

types.title: Types
types.description: TypeScript types that define the accepted values for FAB Button inputs.

item.title: FabButtonItem
item.description: Public interface that defines each menu action. All fields are optional: the only requirement is that the item be identifiable (via id) and have at least an icon or a label.

itemResolved.title: FabButtonItemResolved
itemResolved.description: Internal version of FabButtonItem enriched with four computed fields that the component injects as CSS variables on each <li>. This is the type received in the fabItem template context via let-item.

directives.title: Template directives
directives.description: Standalone directives that allow projecting custom templates into the trigger and into each FAB Button item. They are imported and declared like any Angular standalone directive.

config.title: Global configuration (FabButtonConfig)
config.description: All global configuration properties available via provideNuiConfig({ config: { fabButton: { … } } }). These values are applied when the corresponding component input is not defined.

colors.title: Color variables
colors.description: CSS variables to customize colors by variant (solid, outline, ghost) and semantic color (primary, secondary, etc.).

structure.title: Structural variables
structure.description: CSS variables to customize the radial layout, animations, button sizes, backdrop, tooltips and the focus ring.

tokens.title: Translation tokens
tokens.description: The FabButton component exposes a single i18n token for the accessible label of the trigger button.
tokens.note: Override it with provideNuiI18n({ fabButton: { triggerAriaLabel: 'Open actions' } }).

triggerAriaLabel.description: The aria-label applied to the FAB trigger button. Used by screen readers to identify the button when it only shows an icon.

roles.title: ARIA roles and attributes
roles.description: The FabButton follows the WAI-ARIA Menu Button pattern. The trigger announces the open state and the action list is a proper menu.

roleGroup.description: The FAB host element has role="group" and aria-label so the full widget is announced as a coherent group.

hostLabel.description: The host's aria-label is taken from the i18n token triggerAriaLabel, providing screen reader users context about the purpose of the FAB.

expanded.description: The trigger button has aria-expanded reflecting whether the action list is open (true) or closed (false).

controls.description: The trigger has aria-controls pointing to the id of the actions menu element.

haspopup.description: The trigger has aria-haspopup="menu" to indicate that activating it opens a menu.

ariaHiddenIcon.description: Decorative icons inside action items are hidden with aria-hidden="true".

naming.title: Accessible name
naming.description: The trigger button and each action item must have meaningful accessible names for screen reader users.
naming.note: Always provide a label or tooltip for each action item — these become the accessible name of the menuitem.

trigger.description: The accessible name of the trigger button comes from the i18n token triggerAriaLabel. Override it in your language to reflect the purpose of the FAB.

action.description: The accessible name of each action is extracted from its visible label text or, for icon-only actions, from the tooltip input.

keyboard.title: Keyboard interaction
keyboard.description: The FabButton follows the WAI-ARIA Menu Button keyboard pattern.

enterSpace.description: Activates the trigger button, toggling the actions menu open or closed.

escape.description: Closes the actions menu and returns focus to the trigger button.

arrowDownUp.description: Moves focus between action items within the open menu.

homeEnd.description: Moves focus to the first / last action item in the open menu.

tab.description: Closes the actions menu and moves focus to the next focusable element on the page.

setup.title: Global Configuration
setup.description: The FabButton component supports global configuration via provideNUI(). This allows you to define default values once at the application level, avoiding repetition.
setup.note: Global configuration acts as default values and can always be overridden per instance using component inputs.

defaults.title: Configurable properties
defaults.description: The following properties can be configured globally via FabButtonGlobalConfig:

rows.color: Default color palette applied to the FAB trigger and action items.
rows.variant: Default visual variant applied to all items.
rows.direction: Default direction in which action items expand from the trigger.
rows.animation: Default animation style when opening/closing the actions menu.
rows.layout: Default layout type controlling how items are arranged.
rows.shape: Default shape (circular, square, etc.) of the trigger button.
rows.radius: Default orbit radius for radial layouts.
rows.itemDisplay: Whether action items show an icon, label or both by default.
rows.backdrop: Whether a backdrop is shown behind the open FAB by default.
rows.openOn: Whether the FAB opens on click or hover.
rows.closeOnItemClick: Whether the menu closes automatically when an action item is clicked.
rows.closeOnOutsideClick: Whether the menu closes when the user clicks outside.
rows.closeOnEsc: Whether the menu closes when the Escape key is pressed.
rows.closeOnScroll: Whether the menu closes when the page is scrolled.
rows.triggerIconOpen: Icon class displayed on the trigger button when the menu is open.
rows.triggerLabel: Default label displayed on the trigger button.

priority.title: Resolution priority
priority.description: NUI resolves configuration values using the following priority chain (highest wins):
